---
format:
  html:
    self-contained: true
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{python}
import sys
from pathlib import Path

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# Find project root and make src/ importable
# ------------------------------------------------------------
PROJECT_DIR = Path.cwd()

while not (PROJECT_DIR / "src").exists() and PROJECT_DIR != PROJECT_DIR.parent:
    PROJECT_DIR = PROJECT_DIR.parent

if not (PROJECT_DIR / "src").exists():
    raise FileNotFoundError("Could not find project root containing src/ folder.")

sys.path.insert(0, str(PROJECT_DIR))

import yaml
import folium
import pandas as pd
from IPython.display import display, HTML

from src.cb3_utils import load_cb3_tract_universe
from src.map_utils import (
    add_categorical_point_layer,
    add_demographic_backdrop_layers,
    add_map_title,
    build_grouped_bubble_map,
    build_metric_bubble_map,
    make_base_map,
)

DOMAIN = "education"

SCHOOL_LEVEL_PATH = PROJECT_DIR / "data" / "clean" / "EDUCATION_DOMAIN_school_level_analytic_file.csv"
BASELINE_PROFILE_PATH = PROJECT_DIR / "data" / "clean" / "cb3_tract_baseline_profile_clean.csv"
CLEAN_OUTPUT_PATH = PROJECT_DIR / "data" / "clean" / "education.csv"
METADATA_PATH = PROJECT_DIR / "docs" / "metric_metadata.yml"
MAP_OUTPUT_DIR = PROJECT_DIR / "maps" / "education"
MAP_OUTPUT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
```


## Page-level CSS

```{python}
# Hide Quarto title chrome and shrink Leaflet controls, matching the other
# domain map notebooks.
display(
    HTML(
        """
        <style>
        h1.title { display: none; }
        .quarto-title-block { display: none; }
        .cell-output-stdout { display: none; }
        .leaflet-control-container .legend,
        .leaflet-control-container .branca,
        .leaflet-control-container .colormap,
        .leaflet-control-container .leaflet-control {
            font-size: 12px;
        }
        .education-map-title {
            font-family: Arial, sans-serif;
            font-size: 18px;
            font-weight: 700;
            margin-top: 24px;
            margin-bottom: 8px;
        }
        </style>
        """
    )
)
```


## Load the official 31-tract CB3 universe, geometry, and demographic backdrop

```{python}
tracts_lookup, cb3_tract_geometry, CB3_TRACT_CODES, CB3_GEOIDS = load_cb3_tract_universe(PROJECT_DIR)

# Demographic backdrop layers (income, race/ethnicity, etc.) are shared across
# domain notebooks, matching environment_maps.qmd's pattern, so school points
# can be read against the same selectable choropleth context as other domains.
baseline_profile = pd.read_csv(BASELINE_PROFILE_PATH, dtype={"geoid": "string"})
baseline_profile["GEOID"] = baseline_profile["geoid"].str.zfill(11)

DEMOGRAPHIC_COLUMNS = [
    "GEOID",
    "median_household_income",
    "age_0_to_19_share",
    "age_20_to_64_share",
    "age_65_plus_share",
    "white_non_hispanic_share",
    "black_non_hispanic_share",
    "asian_non_hispanic_share",
    "hispanic_share",
    "poverty_rate",
    "lep_household_share",
]

tracts = cb3_tract_geometry.merge(
    tracts_lookup[["GEOID", "tract_label", "nta_name"]],
    on="GEOID",
    how="left",
    validate="one_to_one",
)
tracts = tracts.merge(
    baseline_profile[DEMOGRAPHIC_COLUMNS],
    on="GEOID",
    how="left",
    validate="one_to_one",
)

assert len(tracts) == 31, f"Expected 31 CB3 tracts, got {len(tracts)}"
assert tracts["GEOID"].is_unique, "GEOID is not unique after merge."
```


## Load metric metadata and build demographic specs

```{python}
with open(METADATA_PATH) as f:
    ALL_METRICS = yaml.safe_load(f)


def _to_map_spec(meta, include_filename=False):
    """Build an add_metric_layer/add_bubble_layer spec dict from one
    metric_metadata.yml entry."""
    spec = {
        "dimension": meta["short_label"],
        "label": meta["label"],
        "unit": meta["unit"],
        "palette": meta["palette"],
    }
    if include_filename:
        spec["filename"] = meta["filename"]
    for key in ("positive_values_only", "zero_color", "zero_label"):
        if key in meta:
            spec[key] = meta[key]
    return spec


# senior_poverty_rate is registered as a demographic metric for other domains'
# notebooks (e.g. seniors), but is not featured as a backdrop layer here,
# matching environment_maps.qmd's exclusion.
EXCLUDED_DEMOGRAPHIC_METRICS = {"senior_poverty_rate"}

DEMOGRAPHIC_SPECS = {
    var: _to_map_spec(meta)
    for var, meta in ALL_METRICS.items()
    if meta["domain"] == "demographics" and var not in EXCLUDED_DEMOGRAPHIC_METRICS
}

METRIC_SPECS = {
    var: _to_map_spec(meta, include_filename=True)
    for var, meta in ALL_METRICS.items()
    if meta.get("domain") == DOMAIN and "filename" in meta and "palette" in meta
}

for metric in DEMOGRAPHIC_SPECS:
    assert metric in tracts.columns, f"{metric} missing from tracts"
```


## Load school-level metrics and flag any schools outside the 31 CB3 tracts

```{python}
def load_school_metrics(path, cb3_geoids):
    """Load the school-level file and assign each school a tract GEOID.

    All CB3 schools are in New York County (state 36, county 061), so GEOID is
    built directly from the school-level `tract` column (zero-padded to 6
    digits) rather than from state/county columns, which are not present in
    this file. Schools whose tract code does not match one of the 31 CB3
    tracts are flagged and excluded, since we do not have coordinates to
    geocode them as a fallback (though every remaining school already has its
    own native `lat`/`long`, so no centroid or spatial join is needed for the
    point maps below).
    """
    frame = pd.read_csv(path)
    frame["GEOID"] = "36061" + frame["tract"].astype(int).astype(str).str.zfill(6)

    unmatched = frame.loc[~frame["GEOID"].isin(cb3_geoids)]
    if len(unmatched):
        display(
            HTML(
                "<p><strong>Flagged:</strong> "
                f"{len(unmatched)} school(s) have a tract code that does not match "
                "any of the 31 CB3 tracts and are excluded below (see 'name', "
                "'tract' columns):</p>"
                + unmatched[["name", "tract", "GEOID"]].to_html(index=False)
            )
        )

    return frame.loc[frame["GEOID"].isin(cb3_geoids)].copy()


school_metrics = load_school_metrics(SCHOOL_LEVEL_PATH, CB3_GEOIDS)
```

**Summary:** Assigns each school a GEOID from its `tract` column and drops
schools that don't match a CB3 tract, displaying them for review. Verify:
check the flagged table above — currently one school (tract code `4000`,
which does not match the split tracts `4001`/`4002`) is expected to appear
and is excluded rather than guessed at.


## Classify each school's enrollment type

```{python}
# The school-level analytic file's own `type` column already distinguishes
# general admission, charter, special education, and transfer schools, so no
# additional data source is needed.
ENROLLMENT_TYPE_LABELS = {
    "General Academic": "General admission",
    "General Academic - Charter": "General admission - charter",
    "Special Education": "Special education",
    "Transfer School": "Transfer school",
}


def classify_enrollment_type(type_value):
    """Map the raw `type` column value to its display label for the
    enrollment-type map layer."""
    return ENROLLMENT_TYPE_LABELS[type_value]
```

**Summary:** Renames the school-level file's existing `type` values (General
Academic, General Academic - Charter, Special Education, Transfer School)
into display labels for the enrollment-type map layer. Verify: confirm every
school's `type` value is one of the 4 keys in `ENROLLMENT_TYPE_LABELS` (a
`KeyError` below would mean a new/unexpected `type` value showed up in the
source file).


## Build the school-level clean table

```{python}
def build_education_school_table(school_frame, tracts_lookup):
    """Attach tract/NTA labels for tooltips and select the columns needed for
    the school-level point maps and clean CSV export.

    Schools are the unit of analysis now (not tracts), since PTA fundraising,
    students in temporary housing, PreK/3-K seats, and IEP rate are
    properties of an individual school, not something meaningfully summed
    across a tract.
    """
    combined = school_frame.merge(
        tracts_lookup[["GEOID", "tract_label", "nta_name"]],
        on="GEOID",
        how="left",
        validate="many_to_one",
    )
    # pct_iep_2122 arrives as a "21.2%"-style string; strip the percent sign
    # to get a plottable numeric rate.
    combined["pct_iep_2122"] = combined["pct_iep_2122"].str.rstrip("%").astype(float)
    # District 75 (admin_district 75) serves students with significant
    # disabilities; flagged separately since a very high IEP rate there
    # reflects that specialized mission rather than being comparable to a
    # general-education school's rate.
    combined["is_d75"] = combined["admin_district"].eq(75)
    combined["enrollment_type"] = combined["type"].map(classify_enrollment_type)
    columns = [
        "GEOID", "name", "tract_label", "nta_name", "lat", "long", "is_d75",
        "enrollment_type",
        "pta_tot_inc", "n_students_temp_housing", "prek_seats_2122", "threek_seats_2122",
        "pct_iep_2122",
    ]
    return combined[columns]


education_schools = build_education_school_table(school_metrics, tracts_lookup)
education_schools.to_csv(CLEAN_OUTPUT_PATH, index=False)
```

**Summary:** Merges each school with its tract/NTA label and writes the
41-row (one per matched school), GEOID-tagged table to
`data/clean/education.csv` — no aggregation, since each row is already a
single school with its own coordinates. Verify: confirm the output has one
row per school (41 rows) and that `lat`/`long` are populated for every row.


## Create one point map per metric

```{python}
# District 75 schools serve students with significant disabilities, so their
# IEP rate isn't comparable to a general-education school's — flagged as a
# separate categorical overlay on the IEP rate map rather than folded into
# the bubble layer, matching environment_maps.qmd's NYCHA-building overlay
# pattern.
D75_COLOR = {"District 75 school": "#252525"}
d75_points = education_schools.loc[education_schools["is_d75"]].assign(
    category="District 75 school"
)


def add_d75_overlay(map_object, show=True):
    add_categorical_point_layer(
        map_object,
        d75_points,
        "category",
        D75_COLOR,
        name="District 75 schools",
        lat_col="lat",
        lon_col="long",
        tooltip_fields=["name", "tract_label"],
        tooltip_aliases=["School", "Census tract"],
        show=show,
    )


def create_school_metric_map(metric, extra_layers=None):
    """Build and save a bubble map for one education metric, with schools
    plotted at their own coordinates over a selectable demographic backdrop,
    matching environment_maps.qmd's point-metric pattern.
    """
    spec = METRIC_SPECS[metric]

    display(
        HTML(
            f"""
            <div class="education-map-title">
                {spec["label"]}
            </div>
            """
        )
    )

    m = build_metric_bubble_map(
        tracts,
        METRIC_SPECS,
        DEMOGRAPHIC_SPECS,
        metric,
        title=f"CB3 Education: {spec['dimension']}",
        output_path=MAP_OUTPUT_DIR / spec["filename"],
        points=education_schools,
        lat_col="lat",
        lon_col="long",
        tooltip_fields=["name", "tract_label"],
        tooltip_aliases=["School", "Census tract"],
        extra_layers=extra_layers,
    )
    display(m)
    return m


maps = {
    metric: create_school_metric_map(
        metric, extra_layers=add_d75_overlay if metric == "pct_iep_2122" else None
    )
    for metric in METRIC_SPECS
}
```


## Combined PreK vs 3-K seats map

```{python}
# PreK and 3-K are different programs (see metric_metadata.yml), so they get
# their own individual maps above, plus this combined view with both as
# selectable bubble layers on one map for side-by-side comparison, matching
# housing_and_affordability_maps.qmd's Class B vs Class C pattern.
prek_and_threek_output_path = MAP_OUTPUT_DIR / "prek_and_threek_seats.html"

display(
    HTML(
        """
        <div class="education-map-title">
            PreK vs 3-K seats
        </div>
        """
    )
)

prek_and_threek_map = build_grouped_bubble_map(
    tracts,
    METRIC_SPECS,
    DEMOGRAPHIC_SPECS,
    ["prek_seats_2122", "threek_seats_2122"],
    title="CB3 Education: PreK vs 3-K seats",
    subtitle=(
        "Choropleth: use the layer control to pick a demographic fill. Bubbles: "
        "size shows seats at each school, split into two selectable layers by "
        "program (PreK, 3-K)."
    ),
    output_path=prek_and_threek_output_path,
    points=education_schools,
    lat_col="lat",
    lon_col="long",
    tooltip_fields=["name", "tract_label"],
    tooltip_aliases=["School", "Census tract"],
)
display(prek_and_threek_map)
```


## School enrollment type map

```{python}
# Categorical, not a magnitude, so this is its own point layer (no bubble
# sizing) built directly from the generic layer helpers, matching
# environment_maps.qmd's street-tree-health map.
ENROLLMENT_TYPE_COLORS = ALL_METRICS["enrollment_type"]["category_colors"]
ENROLLMENT_TYPE_OUTPUT_PATH = MAP_OUTPUT_DIR / ALL_METRICS["enrollment_type"]["filename"]

display(
    HTML(
        """
        <div class="education-map-title">
            School enrollment type
        </div>
        """
    )
)


def create_enrollment_type_map():
    map_object = make_base_map(tracts)
    add_demographic_backdrop_layers(map_object, tracts, DEMOGRAPHIC_SPECS)
    add_categorical_point_layer(
        map_object,
        education_schools,
        "enrollment_type",
        ENROLLMENT_TYPE_COLORS,
        name="School enrollment type",
        lat_col="lat",
        lon_col="long",
        tooltip_fields=["name", "tract_label"],
        tooltip_aliases=["School", "Census tract"],
        show=True,
    )
    add_map_title(
        map_object,
        "CB3 Education: School Enrollment Type",
        "Choropleth: use the layer control to pick a demographic fill. Points: each "
        "school colored by enrollment type (General admission, General admission - "
        "charter, Special education, or Transfer school).",
    )
    folium.LayerControl(collapsed=False, position="topright").add_to(map_object)
    map_object.save(ENROLLMENT_TYPE_OUTPUT_PATH)
    return map_object


enrollment_type_map = create_enrollment_type_map()
display(enrollment_type_map)
```


## Output validation

```{python}
expected_map_files = [
    MAP_OUTPUT_DIR / spec["filename"] for spec in METRIC_SPECS.values()
] + [
    prek_and_threek_output_path,
    ENROLLMENT_TYPE_OUTPUT_PATH,
]

for map_file in expected_map_files:
    assert map_file.exists()
    assert map_file.stat().st_size > 10_000

print(f"Wrote {len(expected_map_files)} map files to {MAP_OUTPUT_DIR}")
```
